semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all eleven exact source mappings, receipt schema and four-scenario probe, three-seed/twelve-arm small-model update result, 24-surface/15-transaction full-state bridge, eight-stage/82-route custody lifecycle, 96 route mutations, three cross-stage mutations, the combined route and two composition theorems, all fifteen stable proof targets, every manifest atom, all sixty-four current prose candidates, test plan, source crosswalk, summary, and handoff. The sweep preserves the Data-and-Descendant Custody Lease as the distinct owner, the no-change update disposition, zero storage erasure, and zero chapter-core effect; separates behavioral, influence, privacy, lineage, legal, storage, backup, and external-descendant claims; and freezes the natural matched-policy, full-state, causal, erasure, reproduction, and transfer campaign.

scope.population: Named data or cohorts, consumers, purposes, learning lanes, rights envelopes, partitions, policies, models, checkpoints, state surfaces, deletion requests, descendants, claim axes, institutions, environments, and times.
scope.environment: Eleven exact mappings; one schema and four-scenario deterministic probe; one three-seed/twelve-arm small-model update result; 24 declared state surfaces and 15/15 local restorations; behavior changes 4, 0, and 1; three lineage propagations; fifteen stable proof targets; and one eight-stage/82-route lifecycle with 96/96 mutation rejection. Influence is unestablished, source storage remains retained, storage erasure is zero, and no privacy, legal, backup, external-descendant, production, reproduction, or transfer closure exists.
scope.model: A scoped Data-and-Descendant Custody Lease over admission, rights, lineage, partitions, competing learning policies, full state, prospective checkpoint authority, deletion propagation, eight independent claim axes, verification, rollback, monitoring, expiry, and terminal custody.
scope.authority: This chapter owns data and descendant custody only. Policy optimization, artifact and supply-chain custody, benchmarks, context and memory, evidence and formalization, capability and readiness, runtime security, normative governance, resources, incidents, release, and publication retain their boundaries.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-16; changes to data, rights, partitions, transformations, state, models, checkpoints, descendants, claims, consumers, institutions, environments, or time expire affected leases and require versioned readmission.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest; eleven mappings; admission schema, fixture, and result; three-seed/twelve-arm update result; 24-surface bridge; eight-stage lifecycle model, independent consumer, schema, result, receipt, and adequacy dossier; fifteen stable targets; semantic review; and future natural independent causal-erasure campaign.

assumptions: Data identity, provenance, rights, transformations, partitions, exposures, state, descendants, deletion authority, and claim axes can be observed and versioned.
Policies, algorithms, seeds, budgets, stopping, checkpoint selection, rollback, deletion, verification, and residual criteria can be frozen prospectively.
Strong replacement, accumulation, replay, reweighting, synthetic, quarantine, retraining, sharding, fine-tuning, and no-change baselines can receive matched opportunity.
Behavior, causal influence, privacy leakage, lineage, legal compliance, storage, backup, and external-descendant closure can be measured without cross-promotion.
Independent trainers, custodians, privacy evaluators, storage verifiers, institutions, and transfer sites can reproduce results.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: A custody lease adds expensive metadata and process without reliably improving data quality, continual learning, forgetting, privacy, deletion closure, recovery, or transfer beyond ordinary dataset, retraining, and storage practice.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: Prospectively frozen natural campaigns show no unique custody or recovery benefit, unacceptable utility or cost, incomplete state and descendant discovery, invalid claim-axis measures, ineffective rollback or deletion, regrowth, or no independent reproduction and transfer.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze provenance, metadata, synthetic-data, continual-learning, and unlearning comparators without importing results.
Justify rights, affected parties, purposes, retention, deletion authority, exceptions, appeal, legal claims, and terminal custody.
Specify finite admission, state, authority, and claim-separation consequences without claiming learning, influence, privacy, or erasure.
Run real ingestion, learning, state discovery, checkpoints, deletion, descendants, replicas, backups, rollback, and recovery.
Measure coverage, leakage, poisoning, utility, tails, forgetting, privacy, sequential deletion, erasure, regrowth, latency, and cost.
Ablate data, policies, transformations, checkpoints, deletion methods, state surfaces, descendants, and verifier assumptions under matched opportunity.
Reproduce independently across data regimes, models, languages, domains, institutions, storage systems, descendants, and time.

contrary_evidence: Zero of nine eligible challenger seed-arms met the registered 0.05 target-utility gain, so the update disposition is no_change.
Deletion-aware behavioral changes were only 4, 0, and 1; influence remains a proxy, source storage remains retained, and privacy, legal, backup, and external-descendant erasure were not shown.
The receipt probe is four deterministic synthetic scenarios and cannot establish provenance truth, data fitness, contamination freedom, lawful use, or learning value.

The fifteen legacy declarations and one combined lifecycle theorem remain authored finite-route consequences; the independent consumer binds existing local results but does not turn them into learning, forgetting, privacy, erasure, recovery, reproduction, or transfer evidence.
Eleven mappings include external and source-reported methods and results that were not reproduced locally.
known_confounds: Data composition, duplicates, partitions, leakage, preprocessing, model capacity, optimization, seeds, checkpoints, evaluator choices, and compute can dominate learning outcomes.
Shared trainers, custodians, storage systems, privacy evaluators, datasets, institutions, or analysis can create false independence.
Outcome-aware split changes, checkpoint selection, deletion target changes, metric tuning, hidden failures, and discarded descendants create selection bias.
Behavior, influence, privacy, lineage, legal, storage, backup, and external closure can diverge while one favorable axis masks the rest.
Custody, retention, full-state, erasure, and audit requirements can conflict and impose large utility, storage, review, privacy, environmental, and affected-party costs.
acceptance_criteria: Every lease binds exact data, provenance, rights, transformations, partitions, competing policy, full state, checkpoint authority, deletion scope, claim axes, descendants, verification, rollback, monitoring, residuals, expiry, and terminal custody.

Natural workloads run with frozen complete partitions and denominators, strong matched policy arms, hidden tests, sequential deletions, adversarial leakage and poisoning controls, tails, and total costs.
Effect-complete rollback and deletion verify declared local and external state, replicas, backups, regenerating processes, descendants, publications, evidence, and residuals.

Any promoted claim survives causal ablation, independent trainer/custodian/privacy/storage/institution reproduction, and heterogeneous transfer without rights, selection, state, claim-axis, or cost laundering.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact data, cohort, provenance, rights, partitions, policy, model, checkpoint, state inventory, deletion request, claim axis, verifier, descendants, consumer, institution, environment, and time.

Separate requested, admitted, quarantined, trained, selected, restored, behavior-changed, influence-reduced, privacy-reduced, invalidated, legally adjudicated, storage-erased, backup-erased, externally closed, reproduced, transferred, and SOTA states.
Unknown provenance truth, rights, contamination, state completeness, causality, privacy, erasure, regrowth, descendants, cost, reproduction, transfer, or residual remains blocked.
refutation_criteria: Refute a custody or deletion result when data identity, rights, partitions, state, checkpoint authority, descendants, verifier, rollback, or lineage is materially invalid.

Refute a policy benefit when matched competitors preserve or improve outcomes or when leakage, selection, extra resources, hidden failures, tail loss, retained influence, regrowth, or omitted costs explain the gain.

Refute forgetting, privacy, legal, storage, safety, readiness, deployment, support, transfer, or SOTA language when only receipts, source reports, finite routes, behavior changes, invalidations, or local digest matches exist.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise custody owner supersedes the atom and every datum, right, transformation, partition, state, descendant, deletion request, claim-axis result, correction, and historical record migrates with lineage.

reproduction.environment_lock: Local admission schema/result, three-seed/twelve-arm small-model update result, 24-surface bridge, eight-stage lifecycle model, independent consumer, and validators current through 2026-07-16; natural independent causal-erasure campaign not frozen or run.
reproduction.evaluator_identity: Current trainers, fixtures, validators, state inventory, and formalization are repository-local; independent trainers, custodians, privacy evaluators, storage verifiers, institutions, and transfer sites are required.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze and run natural heterogeneous learning and deletion workloads with matched strong policy arms, complete partitions and state, causal influence and privacy measures, sequential deletion, replica and backup erasure, regrowth and external-descendant closure, effect-complete rollback, joint utility and cost, independent reproduction, and transfer.

non_claims: No receipt, checksum, provenance graph, metadata record, exclusion, invalidation, behavior change, benchmark score, rollback match, source mapping, finite theorem, or synthetic campaign establishes data fitness, forgetting, influence reduction, privacy, legal or storage erasure, safety, readiness, deployment, support, transfer, or SOTA.

Influence is proxy-only, source storage remains retained, and no privacy, legal, backup, external-descendant, production-custody, natural-task-transfer, independent reproduction, or core-promotion result exists.
The argument-exit campaign is a required attempt specification, not a completed learning or deletion result.

data-engines-continual-learning-and-unle.strongest_counterclaim: Custody leases add overhead without unique learning, deletion, recovery, or transfer value.
data-engines-continual-learning-and-unle.falsifier: Natural matched campaigns show no unique benefit, incomplete state or descendants, ineffective deletion or rollback, unacceptable cost, or no independent reproduction and transfer.
